impacts.statement: Water reaches instrument shelf in gage house.
This is the RECORD FLOOD LEVEL that was reached on August 3 1993.  At this level buildings in Bedford begin to flood.
Buildings in Eldred begin to flood.
The levee on Hurricane Creek in Hillview is overtopped at this stage.
Floodwater reaches the floor the gage house.
The Hillview Drainage District levee and Eldred levee are overtopped near this height.
The Hartwell Levee will be overtopped at this river stage.  Also...Illinois Route 100 at Illinois Route 108 begins flooding at the Kampsville Ferry near this height.
Nutwood levee is overtopped near this height.
Lorton River House and other Hardin businesses along Highway 100 begin flooding near this level.
The U.S. Coast Guard will consider closing the Illinois River to all vessel traffic between 10 and 50 miles above the mouth of the river.
Flood fighting activities begin in Hillview.
Illinois River Road, or Highway 1, in South Hardin begins flooding.
Illinois State Highway 100 north of Kampsville begins flooding and is closed near this height.
Route 100 is flooded north of Hardin.  Jersey County Grain Company utility buildings begin flooding.
Route 96 is overtopped in Kampsville.  The Kampsville Fire Department begins flooding.
River Road in Bedford is overtopped.  In Kampsville, the intersection of Illinois 100 and Illinois 96 floods near this height.
Kampsville Inn begins flooding near this level.
In Kampsville, an open-sided pavillion at the south end of 2nd Street, nearby restrooms, and businesses along Broadway near Highway 96 begin flooding near this height.
The parking lot of the Barefoot Restaurant in Hardin begins flooding near this level.
The sales office at Hurley Dodge in Hardin begins flooding.
Cottages along South Park Street in south Hardin begin flooding near this level.
Water Street in Hardin begins flooding near John F. Kennedy Street.
The Brussels ferry service is interrupted.
Flood Stage.  Lowland flooding of unprotected agricultural land begins.
At and below this height, barge traffic will need to consider lowering tonnage to avoid grounding.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.
